119	
120	  # Image — container image operations (rbw-i, UPPER=mutates, lower=read)
121	  # Three-domain symmetric: hallmarks (h), reliquaries (r), enshrinements (e).
122	  # Verbs: rekon (member-list), muster (catalog-list), Jettison (delete), wrest (pull).
123	  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_IMAGE      "rbw-i"   "Image — Container image operations"
124	  z_mod="rbfl_cli.sh"
125	  buz_enroll RBZ_REKON_HALLMARK         "rbw-irh" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_rekon_hallmark"        ""  "List ark basenames present under a hallmark's GAR subtree"
126	  buz_enroll RBZ_REKON_RELIQUARY        "rbw-irr" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_rekon_reliquary"       ""  "List tool images present under a reliquary stamp's GAR subtree"
127	  buz_enroll RBZ_MUSTER_HALLMARKS       "rbw-imh" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_muster_hallmarks"      ""  "Muster hallmarks — list all hallmark identifiers"
128	  buz_enroll RBZ_MUSTER_RELIQUARIES     "rbw-imr" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_muster_reliquaries"    ""  "Muster reliquaries — list all reliquary stamps"
129	  buz_enroll RBZ_MUSTER_ENSHRINEMENTS   "rbw-ime" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_muster_enshrinements"  ""  "Muster enshrinements — list all enshrined base anchors"
130	  buz_enroll RBZ_WREST_HALLMARK_IMAGE   "rbw-iwh" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_wrest"                 ""  "Wrest a hallmark image from registry"
131	  buz_enroll RBZ_WREST_RELIQUARY_IMAGE  "rbw-iwr" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_wrest"                 ""  "Wrest a reliquary tool image from registry"
132	  buz_enroll RBZ_WREST_ENSHRINED_IMAGE  "rbw-iwe" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_wrest"                 ""  "Wrest an enshrined base image from registry"
133	  buz_enroll RBZ_JETTISON_HALLMARK_IMAGE  "rbw-iJh" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_jettison"            ""  "Jettison a hallmark image tag from registry"
134	  buz_enroll RBZ_JETTISON_RELIQUARY_IMAGE "rbw-iJr" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_jettison"            ""  "Jettison a reliquary tool image from registry"
135	  buz_enroll RBZ_JETTISON_ENSHRINEMENT    "rbw-iJe" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_jettison"            ""  "Jettison an enshrinement from registry"
136	
137	  # Marshal — lifecycle (rbw-M)
138	  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_MARSHAL    "rbw-M"   "Marshal — Lifecycle"
139	  buz_enroll RBZ_MARSHAL_ZERO           "rbw-MZ"  "rblm_cli.sh" "rblm_zero"      ""  "Zero regime to blank template"
140	  buz_enroll RBZ_MARSHAL_PROOF          "rbw-MP"  "rblm_cli.sh" "rblm_proof"     ""  "Proof repo for release testing"
141	  buz_enroll RBZ_MARSHAL_GENERATE       "rbw-MG"  "rblm_cli.sh" "rblm_generate"  ""  "Generate tabtarget context from zipper registry"
142	
143	  # Nameplate — cross-nameplate operations (rbw-n)
144	  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_NAMEPLATE  "rbw-n"   "Nameplate — Cross-nameplate operations"
145	  z_mod="rbrn_cli.sh"
146	  buz_enroll RBZ_LIST_NAMEPLATES        "rbw-rnl" "${z_mod}" "rbrn_list"    ""  "List all nameplates"
147	  buz_enroll RBZ_SURVEY_NAMEPLATES      "rbw-ni"  "${z_mod}" "rbrn_survey"  ""  "Survey nameplate status"
148	  buz_enroll RBZ_AUDIT_NAMEPLATES       "rbw-nv"  "${z_mod}" "rbrn_audit"   ""  "Validate all nameplates"
149	
150	  # Regime — config files (rbw-r)
151	  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_REGIME     "rbw-r"   "Regime — Config files"
152	  z_mod="rbrn_cli.sh"
153	  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_NAMEPLATE       "rbw-rnr" "${z_mod}" "rbrn_render"    "param1"  "Render nameplate regime"
154	  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_NAMEPLATE     "rbw-rnv" "${z_mod}" "rbrn_validate"  "param1"  "Validate nameplate regime"
155	  z_mod="rbrv_cli.sh"
156	  buz_enroll RBZ_LIST_VESSELS           "rbw-rvl" "${z_mod}" "rbrv_list"      ""        "List vessel regimes"
157	  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_VESSEL          "rbw-rvr" "${z_mod}" "rbrv_render"    "param1"  "Render vessel regime"
158	  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_VESSEL        "rbw-rvv" "${z_mod}" "rbrv_validate"  "param1"  "Validate vessel regime"
159	  z_mod="rbrr_cli.sh"
160	  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_REPO            "rbw-rrr" "${z_mod}" "rbrr_render"    ""  "Render repo regime"
161	  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_REPO          "rbw-rrv" "${z_mod}" "rbrr_validate"  ""  "Validate repo regime"
162	  z_mod="rbrp_cli.sh"
163	  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_PAYOR           "rbw-rpr" "${z_mod}" "rbrp_render"    ""  "Render payor regime"
164	  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_PAYOR         "rbw-rpv" "${z_mod}" "rbrp_validate"  ""  "Validate payor regime"
165	  z_mod="rbro_cli.sh"
166	  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_OAUTH           "rbw-ror" "${z_mod}" "rbro_render"    ""  "Render OAuth regime"
167	  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_OAUTH         "rbw-rov" "${z_mod}" "rbro_validate"  ""  "Validate OAuth regime"
168	  z_mod="rbrs_cli.sh"
169	  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_STATION         "rbw-rsr" "${z_mod}" "rbrs_render"    ""  "Render station regime"
170	  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_STATION       "rbw-rsv" "${z_mod}" "rbrs_validate"  ""  "Validate station regime"
171	  z_mod="rbra_cli.sh"
172	  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_AUTH            "rbw-rar" "${z_mod}" "rbra_render"    "param1"  "Render auth regime"
173	  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_AUTH          "rbw-rav" "${z_mod}" "rbra_validate"  "param1"  "Validate auth regime"
174	  buz_enroll RBZ_LIST_AUTH              "rbw-ral" "${z_mod}" "rbra_list"      ""        "List auth regimes"
175	
176	  # Theurge — test infrastructure (rbw-t)
177	  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_THEURGE    "rbw-t"   "Theurge — Test infrastructure"
178	  z_mod="rbob_cli.sh"
179	  buz_enroll RBZ_THEURGE_KLUDGE  "rbw-tK"  "${z_mod}" "rbob_kludge"        "imprint"  "Local kludge build + install hallmark into nameplate"
180	  buz_enroll RBZ_THEURGE_ORDAIN  "rbw-tO"  "${z_mod}" "rbob_ordain"        "imprint"  "Cloud build + install hallmark into nameplate"
181	  z_mod="rbq_cli.sh"
182	  buz_enroll RBZ_QUALIFY_FAST    "rbw-tf"   "${z_mod}" "rbq_qualify_fast"   ""         "Fast qualify: tabtargets, colophons, nameplate health"
183	  buz_enroll RBZ_QUALIFY_RELEASE "rbw-tr"   "${z_mod}" "rbq_qualify_release" ""        "Release qualify: + shellcheck, full test suite"
184	
185	  # Handbook — human-facing procedures (rbw-h0 index, rbw-hw/HW* windows)
186	  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_HANDBOOK   "rbw-HW"  "Handbook — Human-facing procedures"
187	  z_mod="rbh0/rbhw0_cli.sh"
188	  buz_enroll RBZ_HANDBOOK_TOP           "rbw-h0"    "${z_mod}" "rbhw_handbook_top"              ""        "Top-level handbook index across all groups"
189	  buz_enroll RBZ_HANDBOOK_WINDOWS       "rbw-hw"    "${z_mod}" "rbhw_top"                       ""        "Windows test infrastructure orchestrator"
190	  buz_enroll RBZ_HW_DOCKER_DESKTOP     "rbw-HWdd"  "${z_mod}" "rbhw_docker_desktop"            ""        "Docker Desktop install"
191	  buz_enroll RBZ_HW_DOCKER_WSL_NATIVE  "rbw-HWdw"  "${z_mod}" "rbhw_docker_wsl_native"         "param1"  "Native dockerd in WSL"
192	  buz_enroll RBZ_HW_DOCKER_CONTEXT     "rbw-HWdc"  "${z_mod}" "rbhw_docker_context_discipline" ""        "Deterministic daemon selection"
193	
194	  readonly ZRBZ_COLOPHON_MANIFEST="${z_buz_colophon_roll[*]}"
195	
196	  readonly ZRBZ_KINDLED=1
197	}
198	
199	######################################################################
200	# Healthcheck (validates all enrolled tabtargets exist on disk)
201	
202	zrbz_healthcheck() {
203	  zrbz_sentinel
204	  buz_healthcheck
205	}
206	
207	######################################################################
208	# Internal sentinel
209	
210	zrbz_sentinel() {
211	  test "${ZRBZ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbz not kindled - call zrbz_kindle first"
212	}
213	
214	# eof
215

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# RBW Zipper - Colophon registry for RBW workbench dispatch

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard
test -z "${ZRBZ_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZRBZ_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Colophon registry initialization

zrbz_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBZ_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "rbz already kindled"

  # Verify buz zipper is kindled (CLI furnish must kindle buz first)
  zbuz_sentinel

  # Accounts — Google Cloud service accounts (rbw-a)
  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_ACCOUNTS   "rbw-a"   "Accounts — Service account management"
  local z_mod="rbgp_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR         "rbw-aM"  "${z_mod}" "rbgp_governor_mantle"       ""        "Create/replace governor service account"
  z_mod="rbgg_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_CHARTER_RETRIEVER       "rbw-aC"  "${z_mod}" "rbgg_charter_retriever"     ""        "Charter retriever (image pull) service account"
  buz_enroll RBZ_KNIGHT_DIRECTOR         "rbw-aK"  "${z_mod}" "rbgg_knight_director"       ""        "Knight director (build) service account"
  buz_enroll RBZ_LIST_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS   "rbw-aL"  "${z_mod}" "rbgg_list_service_accounts" ""        "List issued service accounts"
  buz_enroll RBZ_FORFEIT_SERVICE_ACCOUNT "rbw-aF"  "${z_mod}" "rbgg_forfeit_service_account" ""      "Forfeit a service account"

  # Crucible — container runtime (rbw-c)
  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_CRUCIBLE   "rbw-c"   "Crucible — Container runtime"
  z_mod="rbob_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_CRUCIBLE_CHARGE  "rbw-cC"  "${z_mod}" "rbob_charge"       "imprint"  "Charge crucible (sentry + pentacle + bottle containers)"
  buz_enroll RBZ_CRUCIBLE_QUENCH  "rbw-cQ"  "${z_mod}" "rbob_quench"       "imprint"  "Quench crucible"
  buz_enroll RBZ_CRUCIBLE_SSH     "rbw-cS"  "${z_mod}" "rbob_ssh"          "imprint"  "SSH into the bottle container"
  buz_enroll RBZ_CRUCIBLE_HAIL    "rbw-ch"  "${z_mod}" "rbob_hail"         "param1"   "Shell into the sentry container"
  buz_enroll RBZ_CRUCIBLE_RACK    "rbw-cr"  "${z_mod}" "rbob_rack"         "param1"   "Shell into the bottle container"
  buz_enroll RBZ_CRUCIBLE_SCRY    "rbw-cs"  "${z_mod}" "rbob_scry"         "param1"   "Observe network traffic on crucible containers"
  buz_enroll RBZ_CRUCIBLE_WRIT    "rbw-cw"  "${z_mod}" "rbob_writ"         "imprint"  "Non-interactive exec in sentry container"
  buz_enroll RBZ_CRUCIBLE_FIAT    "rbw-cf"  "${z_mod}" "rbob_fiat"         "imprint"  "Non-interactive exec in pentacle container"
  buz_enroll RBZ_CRUCIBLE_BARK    "rbw-cb"  "${z_mod}" "rbob_bark"         "imprint"  "Non-interactive exec in bottle container"
  buz_enroll RBZ_CRUCIBLE_ACTIVE  "rbw-cic" "${z_mod}" "rbob_charged"      "param1"   "Check if crucible is currently charged"
  buz_enroll RBZ_CRUCIBLE_KLUDGE_BOTTLE "rbw-cKB" "${z_mod}" "rbob_kludge_bottle" "param1" "Kludge bottle vessel and drive hallmark into nameplate"
  buz_enroll RBZ_CRUCIBLE_KLUDGE_SENTRY "rbw-cKS" "${z_mod}" "rbob_kludge_sentry" "param1" "Kludge sentry vessel and drive hallmark into nameplate"

  # Depot — GCP project infrastructure (rbw-d, UPPER=mutates, lower=read)
  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_DEPOT      "rbw-d"   "Depot — GCP project infrastructure"
  z_mod="rbgp_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_LEVY_DEPOT             "rbw-dL"  "${z_mod}" "rbgp_depot_levy"   ""  "Provision GCP depot project"
  buz_enroll RBZ_UNMAKE_DEPOT           "rbw-dU"  "${z_mod}" "rbgp_depot_unmake"  ""  "Permanently remove a depot"
  buz_enroll RBZ_LIST_DEPOT             "rbw-dl"  "${z_mod}" "rbgp_depot_list"    ""  "List all active depots"
  z_mod="rbfd_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_ENSHRINE_VESSEL        "rbw-dE"  "${z_mod}" "rbfd_enshrine"      ""  "Enshrine upstream base images to GAR via Cloud Build"
  z_mod="rbfl_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_INSCRIBE_RELIQUARY     "rbw-dI"  "${z_mod}" "rbfl_inscribe"      ""  "Create reliquary: mirror tool images from upstream to GAR"
  buz_enroll RBZ_YOKE_RELIQUARY         "rbw-dY"  "${z_mod}" "rbfl_yoke"          ""  "Yoke a reliquary stamp into a conjure vessel's rbrv.env"

  # Guide — human-directed procedures (rbw-g)
  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_GUIDE      "rbw-g"   "Guide — Human-directed procedures"
  z_mod="rbgp_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_PAYOR_INSTALL          "rbw-gPI" "${z_mod}" "rbgp_payor_install"   ""  "Ingest OAuth credentials from JSON key file"
  z_mod="rbh0/rbhp0_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_PAYOR_ESTABLISH        "rbw-gPE" "${z_mod}" "rbhp_establish"       ""  "Guided Manor establishment — GCP project + OAuth consent screen"
  buz_enroll RBZ_PAYOR_REFRESH          "rbw-gPR" "${z_mod}" "rbhp_refresh"         ""  "Refresh expired OAuth tokens"
  buz_enroll RBZ_QUOTA_BUILD            "rbw-gq"  "${z_mod}" "rbhp_quota_build"     ""  "Display Cloud Build capacity review procedure"
  # Onboarding — handbook tracks (rbw-o terminal + rbw-O* family, see ₣A6 paddock)
  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_ONBOARDING "rbw-o"   "Onboarding — Handbook restart"
  z_mod="rbh0/rbho0_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE    "rbw-o"   "${z_mod}" "rbho_start_here"            ""  "Onboarding start — probe-aware menu into handbook tracks"
  buz_enroll RBZ_ONBOARD_CRASH_COURSE  "rbw-Occ" "${z_mod}" "rbho_crash_course"          ""  "Crash Course — universal prerequisite: tabtargets, regimes, diagnostic failure"
  buz_enroll RBZ_ONBOARD_CRED_RETRIEVER "rbw-Ocr" "${z_mod}" "rbho_credential_retriever" ""  "Install retriever credentials — place RBRA key file"
  buz_enroll RBZ_ONBOARD_CRED_DIRECTOR  "rbw-Ocd" "${z_mod}" "rbho_credential_director"  ""  "Install director credentials — place RBRA key file"
  buz_enroll RBZ_ONBOARD_FIRST_CRUCIBLE "rbw-Ofc" "${z_mod}" "rbho_first_crucible"       ""  "Start a Crucible using local builds — kludge, charge, rack"
  buz_enroll RBZ_ONBOARD_TADMOR_SECURITY "rbw-Ots" "${z_mod}" "rbho_tadmor_security"     ""  "Verify Crucible containment under attack — charge tadmor and run the adversarial suite"
  buz_enroll RBZ_ONBOARD_DIR_FIRST_BUILD "rbw-Odf" "${z_mod}" "rbho_director_first_build" "" "Your First Cloud Build — inscribe, conjure, tour, summon, abjure"
  buz_enroll RBZ_ONBOARD_DIR_AIRGAP     "rbw-Oda" "${z_mod}" "rbho_director_airgap"      ""  "Airgap Cloud Build — enshrine, conjure forge, conjure airgap, charge moriah, compare plumb"
  buz_enroll RBZ_ONBOARD_DIR_BIND       "rbw-Odb" "${z_mod}" "rbho_director_bind"        ""  "Bind Cloud Build — pin upstream image by digest, mode-mixture pluml Crucible"
  buz_enroll RBZ_ONBOARD_DIR_GRAFT      "rbw-Odg" "${z_mod}" "rbho_director_graft"       ""  "Graft Cloud Build — push locally-built image, inspect GRAFTED Vouch verdict"
  buz_enroll RBZ_ONBOARD_PAYOR_HB      "rbw-Op"  "${z_mod}" "rbho_payor_handbook"       ""  "Payor — establish a Manor and provision the Depot"
  buz_enroll RBZ_ONBOARD_GOVERNOR_HB   "rbw-Og"  "${z_mod}" "rbho_governor_handbook"    ""  "Governor — administer service accounts for directors and retrievers"

  # Foundry — registry artifact lifecycle (rbw-f, UPPER=mutates GAR, lower=read/local)
  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_FOUNDRY    "rbw-f"   "Foundry — Registry artifact lifecycle"
  z_mod="rbfd_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_ORDAIN_HALLMARK        "rbw-fO"  "${z_mod}" "rbfd_ordain"          ""  "Ordain hallmark: conjure, bind, or graft based on vessel mode"

  buz_enroll RBZ_ABJURE_HALLMARK        "rbw-fA"  "${z_mod}" "rbfl_abjure"          ""  "Abjure a hallmark (delete artifacts from GAR)"

  z_mod="rbfv_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_VOUCH_HALLMARKS        "rbw-fV"  "${z_mod}" "rbfv_batch_vouch"     ""  "Mode-aware vouch: SLSA (conjure), digest-pin (bind), GRAFTED (graft)"
  z_mod="rbfr_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_SUMMON_HALLMARK        "rbw-fs"  "${z_mod}" "rbfr_summon"          ""  "Summon vouched hallmark image locally"
  z_mod="rbfc_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_PLUMB_FULL             "rbw-fpf" "${z_mod}" "rbfc_plumb_full"      ""  "Full provenance display (SBOM, build info, Dockerfile)"
  buz_enroll RBZ_PLUMB_COMPACT          "rbw-fpc" "${z_mod}" "rbfc_plumb_compact"   ""  "Compact provenance summary"

  # Ifrit — attack binary (rbw-I)
  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_IFRIT      "rbw-I"   "Ifrit — Attack binary"
  z_mod="rbob_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_BOTTLE_IFRIT   "rbw-Ic"  "${z_mod}" "rbob_ifrit_client"  "imprint"  "Launch Claude Code inside a running bottle for escape testing"
  buz_enroll RBZ_BOTTLE_SORTIE  "rbw-Is"  "${z_mod}" "rbob_ifrit_sortie"  "imprint"  "Run automated security test scripts inside the bottle"

  # Image — container image operations (rbw-i, UPPER=mutates, lower=read)
  # Three-domain symmetric: hallmarks (h), reliquaries (r), enshrinements (e).
  # Verbs: rekon (member-list), muster (catalog-list), Jettison (delete), wrest (pull).
  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_IMAGE      "rbw-i"   "Image — Container image operations"
  z_mod="rbfl_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_REKON_HALLMARK         "rbw-irh" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_rekon_hallmark"        ""  "List ark basenames present under a hallmark's GAR subtree"
  buz_enroll RBZ_REKON_RELIQUARY        "rbw-irr" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_rekon_reliquary"       ""  "List tool images present under a reliquary stamp's GAR subtree"
  buz_enroll RBZ_MUSTER_HALLMARKS       "rbw-imh" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_muster_hallmarks"      ""  "Muster hallmarks — list all hallmark identifiers"
  buz_enroll RBZ_MUSTER_RELIQUARIES     "rbw-imr" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_muster_reliquaries"    ""  "Muster reliquaries — list all reliquary stamps"
  buz_enroll RBZ_MUSTER_ENSHRINEMENTS   "rbw-ime" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_muster_enshrinements"  ""  "Muster enshrinements — list all enshrined base anchors"
  buz_enroll RBZ_WREST_HALLMARK_IMAGE   "rbw-iwh" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_wrest"                 ""  "Wrest a hallmark image from registry"
  buz_enroll RBZ_WREST_RELIQUARY_IMAGE  "rbw-iwr" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_wrest"                 ""  "Wrest a reliquary tool image from registry"
  buz_enroll RBZ_WREST_ENSHRINED_IMAGE  "rbw-iwe" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_wrest"                 ""  "Wrest an enshrined base image from registry"
  buz_enroll RBZ_JETTISON_HALLMARK_IMAGE  "rbw-iJh" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_jettison"            ""  "Jettison a hallmark image tag from registry"
  buz_enroll RBZ_JETTISON_RELIQUARY_IMAGE "rbw-iJr" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_jettison"            ""  "Jettison a reliquary tool image from registry"
  buz_enroll RBZ_JETTISON_ENSHRINEMENT    "rbw-iJe" "${z_mod}" "rbfl_jettison"            ""  "Jettison an enshrinement from registry"

  # Marshal — lifecycle (rbw-M)
  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_MARSHAL    "rbw-M"   "Marshal — Lifecycle"
  buz_enroll RBZ_MARSHAL_ZERO           "rbw-MZ"  "rblm_cli.sh" "rblm_zero"      ""  "Zero regime to blank template"
  buz_enroll RBZ_MARSHAL_PROOF          "rbw-MP"  "rblm_cli.sh" "rblm_proof"     ""  "Proof repo for release testing"
  buz_enroll RBZ_MARSHAL_GENERATE       "rbw-MG"  "rblm_cli.sh" "rblm_generate"  ""  "Generate tabtarget context from zipper registry"

  # Nameplate — cross-nameplate operations (rbw-n)
  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_NAMEPLATE  "rbw-n"   "Nameplate — Cross-nameplate operations"
  z_mod="rbrn_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_LIST_NAMEPLATES        "rbw-rnl" "${z_mod}" "rbrn_list"    ""  "List all nameplates"
  buz_enroll RBZ_SURVEY_NAMEPLATES      "rbw-ni"  "${z_mod}" "rbrn_survey"  ""  "Survey nameplate status"
  buz_enroll RBZ_AUDIT_NAMEPLATES       "rbw-nv"  "${z_mod}" "rbrn_audit"   ""  "Validate all nameplates"

  # Regime — config files (rbw-r)
  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_REGIME     "rbw-r"   "Regime — Config files"
  z_mod="rbrn_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_NAMEPLATE       "rbw-rnr" "${z_mod}" "rbrn_render"    "param1"  "Render nameplate regime"
  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_NAMEPLATE     "rbw-rnv" "${z_mod}" "rbrn_validate"  "param1"  "Validate nameplate regime"
  z_mod="rbrv_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_LIST_VESSELS           "rbw-rvl" "${z_mod}" "rbrv_list"      ""        "List vessel regimes"
  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_VESSEL          "rbw-rvr" "${z_mod}" "rbrv_render"    "param1"  "Render vessel regime"
  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_VESSEL        "rbw-rvv" "${z_mod}" "rbrv_validate"  "param1"  "Validate vessel regime"
  z_mod="rbrr_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_REPO            "rbw-rrr" "${z_mod}" "rbrr_render"    ""  "Render repo regime"
  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_REPO          "rbw-rrv" "${z_mod}" "rbrr_validate"  ""  "Validate repo regime"
  z_mod="rbrp_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_PAYOR           "rbw-rpr" "${z_mod}" "rbrp_render"    ""  "Render payor regime"
  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_PAYOR         "rbw-rpv" "${z_mod}" "rbrp_validate"  ""  "Validate payor regime"
  z_mod="rbro_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_OAUTH           "rbw-ror" "${z_mod}" "rbro_render"    ""  "Render OAuth regime"
  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_OAUTH         "rbw-rov" "${z_mod}" "rbro_validate"  ""  "Validate OAuth regime"
  z_mod="rbrs_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_STATION         "rbw-rsr" "${z_mod}" "rbrs_render"    ""  "Render station regime"
  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_STATION       "rbw-rsv" "${z_mod}" "rbrs_validate"  ""  "Validate station regime"
  z_mod="rbra_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_RENDER_AUTH            "rbw-rar" "${z_mod}" "rbra_render"    "param1"  "Render auth regime"
  buz_enroll RBZ_VALIDATE_AUTH          "rbw-rav" "${z_mod}" "rbra_validate"  "param1"  "Validate auth regime"
  buz_enroll RBZ_LIST_AUTH              "rbw-ral" "${z_mod}" "rbra_list"      ""        "List auth regimes"

  # Theurge — test infrastructure (rbw-t)
  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_THEURGE    "rbw-t"   "Theurge — Test infrastructure"
  z_mod="rbob_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_THEURGE_KLUDGE  "rbw-tK"  "${z_mod}" "rbob_kludge"        "imprint"  "Local kludge build + install hallmark into nameplate"
  buz_enroll RBZ_THEURGE_ORDAIN  "rbw-tO"  "${z_mod}" "rbob_ordain"        "imprint"  "Cloud build + install hallmark into nameplate"
  z_mod="rbq_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_QUALIFY_FAST    "rbw-tf"   "${z_mod}" "rbq_qualify_fast"   ""         "Fast qualify: tabtargets, colophons, nameplate health"
  buz_enroll RBZ_QUALIFY_RELEASE "rbw-tr"   "${z_mod}" "rbq_qualify_release" ""        "Release qualify: + shellcheck, full test suite"

  # Handbook — human-facing procedures (rbw-h0 index, rbw-hw/HW* windows)
  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_HANDBOOK   "rbw-HW"  "Handbook — Human-facing procedures"

  buz_enroll RBZ_HW_DOCKER_CONTEXT     "rbw-HWdc"  "${z_mod}" "rbhw_docker_context_discipline" ""        "Deterministic daemon selection"

  readonly ZRBZ_COLOPHON_MANIFEST="${z_buz_colophon_roll[*]}"

  readonly ZRBZ_KINDLED=1
}

######################################################################
# Healthcheck (validates all enrolled tabtargets exist on disk)

zrbz_healthcheck() {
  zrbz_sentinel
  buz_healthcheck
}

######################################################################
# Internal sentinel

zrbz_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBZ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbz not kindled - call zrbz_kindle first"
}

# eof

4ed6e57c jjb:1011-de3ecb26:₢BBAAN:n: Step C of cult-verb-foundation: swap rbz_zipper.sh enrollments to match the rewritten rbgg_Governor.sh function surface. Delete the four old SA-management enrollments (RBZ_CHARTER_RETRIEVER->rbw-aC, RBZ_KNIGHT_DIRECTOR->rbw-aK, RBZ_LIST_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS->rbw-aL, RBZ_FORFEIT_SERVICE_ACCOUNT->rbw-aF) and add six new enrollments matching the docket's settled colophon scheme: RBZ_INVEST_RETRIEVER->rbw-arI, RBZ_INVEST_DIRECTOR->rbw-adI, RBZ_DIVEST_RETRIEVER->rbw-arD, RBZ_DIVEST_DIRECTOR->rbw-adD, RBZ_ROSTER_RETRIEVERS->rbw-arr, RBZ_ROSTER_DIRECTORS->rbw-adr. Channel discipline matches mount-time decision: invest/divest take operator-supplied identity via param1; roster takes no folio (empty channel string ''). Function references point at the six new external functions just landed in rbgg_Governor.sh (rbgg_invest_retriever, rbgg_invest_director, rbgg_divest_retriever, rbgg_divest_director, rbgg_roster_retrievers, rbgg_roster_directors). Constant-name pluralization follows function-shape: invest/divest singular (single-SA target), roster plural (lists multiple SAs). Letter case in colophons preserves the cult-verb capital=mutating / lower=read-only convention (I, D capitals for cloud-mutating; r lowercase for read-only roster). RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR (rbw-aM) untouched per docket: governor lifecycle stays singleton-per-depot and folds into depot-unmake (Step E) rather than gaining a demantle verb. Tabtargets in tt/ retire and create in Step D; rbgp_depot_unmake fold lands in Step E; tabtarget context regen is Step F. bash -n clean.
de3ecb26 jjb:1011-39d84f11:₢BBAAN:n: Step B of cult-verb-foundation: rewrite rbgg_Governor.sh function surface for the SA-management cult-verb collapse. (1) Delete the four old external functions (rbgg_charter_retriever, rbgg_knight_director, rbgg_list_service_accounts, rbgg_forfeit_service_account). (2) Add six new external functions split per role: rbgg_invest_retriever, rbgg_invest_director, rbgg_divest_retriever, rbgg_divest_director, rbgg_roster_retrievers, rbgg_roster_directors. (3) Two internal helpers consolidate role-symmetry: zrbgg_roster_role(role, fact_ext) drives paginated SA list -> role-prefix filter -> per-identity buf_write_fact_multi emit (basename=identity parsed from local-part by stripping role-prefix; content=full email per mount-time decision); zrbgg_divest_role(role, identity, role_rbra_file) synthesizes email = ${role}-${identity}@${RBGD_SA_EMAIL_FULL}, DELETEs (404-tolerant via existing rbgu_http_require_ok '...' '...' 404 'not found (already deleted)' pattern), and runs opportunistic role-RBRA cleanup that source-loads the installed RBRA in a subshell and rm -f's only when RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL matches the divested email. (4) Rework zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key signature: drop the z_instance arg (5->4 args: account_name, display_name, description, role); credential bytes now write to RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE exclusively (the assay-as-binding-producer-output discipline) instead of ${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${z_instance}.rbra; intermediate decoded key JSON keyed by z_account_name. The operator-facing post-invest message reads 'Install: cp ${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE} ${RBDC_<role>_RBRA_FILE}' (singular path, no fork between production-mode and assay-mode messaging). Invest preexistence remains fatal-per-(role,identity) via the existing 409-fatal kindle posture. (5) BCG repair: three rbgg_Governor.sh:248/252/256 sites and one preflight site (line 193) used `var=$(jq -r ... ZRBGU_file)` which violates BCG:495 (external-tool capture must use temp files; only _capture/_recite functions are permitted on the right-hand side of $()). Repair: relocate the decoded key JSON inside the rbgu cache by composing ${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${z_key_decoded_infix}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON} (with z_key_decoded_infix=${z_key_infix}_decoded) so rbgu_json_field_capture (a sanctioned _capture) can address it by infix; flip all four jq direct calls to rbgu_json_field_capture. The earlier `rm -f ${z_key_json}` at end-of-function also retired (BCG:518 forbids module-level temp file deletion; cleanup is infrastructure's responsibility, and the b64 form was already preserved in the keys.create response file in the same cache anyway, so this is no new exposure). (6) Add `source ${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh` to rbgg_cli.sh:35 furnish chain (between burd_regime.sh and rbcc_Constants.sh) so buf_write_fact_multi is callable inside the new roster paths — same furnish-gap pattern as BBAAM Cycle 3's rbgp_cli.sh fix (functions sourced by bud_dispatch.sh do not survive buz_exec_lookup's exec boundary). zburd_sentinel calls retired from invest functions (no longer touch BURD_OUTPUT_DIR directly; buf_write_fact_multi inside roster relies on furnish-time zburd_kindle, matching rbgp_Payor.sh:311 sibling precedent). bash -n clean across both files. Pace state: function surface settled; consumer wire-up (rbz_zipper enrollments, tabtargets, depot-unmake fold) follows in subsequent steps. Surfaced for separate scope (NOT touched here): rbgg_Governor.sh:341 in zrbgg_list_bucket_objects_capture has the same `$(jq -r ... ZRBGU_file)` pattern (different function, sibling _capture, distinct concern from the SA-management refactor); left for a dedicated BCG sweep — docket discipline for BBAAN is cult-verb refactor, not BCG drift sweep.

content.content:
3c9d38fb jjb:1011-94a014eb:₢BBAAM:n: Step 1 of rbrr-depot-identity-collapse: kindle infrastructure plus mechanical sweep. RBRR drops RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID, RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY, RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM and adds RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER (format ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$, length 1-26). RBDC adds RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID (${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}), RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY (${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar), RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM (${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool), and RBDC_GCS_BUCKET (${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}); type markers and pool/tether/airgap suffixes are literal strings inside RBDC to keep rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering (existing furnishers run rbdc-then-rbgc in most modules, rbgc-then-rbdc in others — uniformity left for a future cleanup). RBGC drops obsolete RBGC_GLOBAL_DEPOT_REGEX and RBGC_GLOBAL_BUCKET_REGEX (project_id and bucket are now installation-prefix-anchored, not timestamp-suffixed) and gains depot-state vocabulary tinder constants (RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE/BROKEN/DELETE_REQUESTED) plus RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT for the per-moniker fact-file shape coming in Step 4. RBGD switches RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID -> RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID throughout, drops the prefix-strip-and-timestamp depot-name extraction logic (RBGD_DEPOT_NAME is now just RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER), and reshapes RBGD_GCS_BUCKET via RBDC_GCS_BUCKET. Marshal-zero (rblm_cli.sh) banner copy and sed cases adjusted to the new field set. Compose bridge: rbob_bottle.sh writes a temp env file (rbob_rbdc_compose.env) at zrbob_kindle with RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID and RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY, added to the docker compose --env-file list; .rbk/rbob_compose.yml image refs switched from RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID/RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY to the RBDC equivalents. Operator's .rbk/rbrr.env reshaped to the new field set with RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER=depot10041 placeholder. Mechanical RBRR_X -> RBDC_X sweep across all consumers: rbgg_Governor, rbgv_AccessProbe, rbgp_Payor (depot-list regex sites replaced with string-prefix match against ${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-, post-levy 'Update RBRR configuration' banner replaced with informational 'Depot resources created' summary, governor-mantle docstring and error message rewritten around the new derivation), rbgp_cli (differential-furnish comment annotated Step-2-pending), rbgb_Buckets, rbga_ArtifactRegistry, rbob_bottle (GAR ref via RBDC), rbrn_cli, rbfv_FoundryVerify, rbfc_FoundryCore, rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild, rbfl_FoundryLedger, rbhpq_quota_build (also RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM -> RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM), rbhpb_base. Onboarding probes that file-scan rbrr.env to detect 'depot configured' switched from RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID -> RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER: rbhocc_crash_course, rbhodf_director_first_build, rbhoda_director_airgap, rbhodb_director_bind, rbhodg_director_graft. Theurge minimal-touch: rbtdrp_pristine.rs RBTDRP_RBRR_BLANK_FIELDS shrinks to {CLOUD_PREFIX, RUNTIME_PREFIX, DEPOT_MONIKER}; helper rbtdrp_install_depot_project_id renamed to rbtdrp_install_depot_moniker (case 3 call site updated, but the ordering is service-tier-broken until Step 5 reshapes case 3). rbtdrf_fast.rs negative tests rbrr-missing-project-id -> rbrr-missing-moniker (unset RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER) and rbrr-bad-pool-stem -> rbrr-bad-moniker (export RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER=BAD-MONIKER triggers format check). Build green: rbtd-b, rbtd-t (58/58 unit tests), rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast (97/97 across enrollment-validation/regime-validation/regime-smoke/handbook-render), vow-b. Step 2 (rbgp_depot_levy reshape — drop timestamp, drop CLI arg, use RBDC, drop post-levy banner) is the next pace; rbgp_depot_levy in this state is left functionally intact with its old CLI-arg flow but its post-creation values can no longer be 'pasted into RBRR' (they're derived now), so the function is effectively unusable until Step 2 lands.
2d8986d2 jjb:1011-c97804d8:₢A_AAS:n: Expand RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX/RUNTIME_PREFIX negative coverage in regime-validation: per-failure-mode decomposition. Each prefix's docket-spec'd failure modes (uppercase / no-trailing-hyphen / too-long, against the rbrr_regime.sh:99-103 enforce regex ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*-$ length 2..=11) get their own case, replacing the prior single-shot bad_cloud_prefix=BAD- and bad_runtime_prefix=acme that each exercised only one mode by accident. Six new cases total; rv-rbrr-negative section now 14 (was 8). RCG Constant Discipline applied: extracted RBTDRF_VAR_RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX/RUNTIME_PREFIX consts (5+/4+ refs after expansion) and threaded through both the negative cases and the rs_rbrr_nonempty_prefix smoke (named-arg format string for the smoke's ${{var}} bash interpolation). Fast suite GREEN: enrollment-validation 47/47, regime-validation 27/27 (was 21), regime-smoke 8/8 (was 7), handbook-render 15/15. Manifest update item from the AAS docket already complete from prior work — RBTDRM_COLOPHON_SUMMON / PLUMB_FULL / PLUMB_COMPACT / VOUCH and RBTDRM_FIXTURE_BATCH_VOUCH all present in rbtdrm_manifest.rs with REKON + JETTISON registered.
7f6cb62e jjb:1011-684f003d:₢A_AAS:n: Theurge coverage catchup: 3 fast-suite prefix cases (90→93 green), four-mode extended with summon/plumb_full/rekon (conjure) and plumb_compact/jettison/post-jettison-rekon (bind), new batch-vouch standalone fixture exercising pending→vouched two-pass via plant-by-vouch-jettison.
97f404c3 jjb:1011-d6e6b964:₢A1AAe:n: Rename domain concept Consecration to Hallmark across entire system — quoin definitions, AsciiDoc specs, bash variables/functions/strings, Rust constants/literals, env files, cloud build jobs, Python scripts, consumer docs. Build passes, 51 unit tests pass, fast suite 75 cases pass.
eb7aaed8 jjb:1011-1792352a:₢A1AAU:n: case!() macro replaces all 126 case registrations with compiler-enforced unique names; single-case runner subcommand and tabtargets for all fixtures
db2b2866 jjb:1011-8623e967:₢A1AAI:n: Port all 3 fast-tier bash test fixtures to theurge: enrollment-validation (47 cases), regime-validation (21 cases), regime-smoke (7 cases). Created rbtdrf_fast.rs with data-driven bash subprocess execution for BUV enrollment tests and regime kindle/enforce tests, plus direct tabtarget invocation for smoke tests. Extended manifest to accept fast fixtures with no colophon requirements. Retired 9 bash case files, 3 bash fixture tabtargets, and testbench enrollments. All 75 cases verified live with parity against bash originals. 51 unit tests pass.

content.text: Let me check whether these failures pre-existed before my BBAAN work.

content.content:
972e1dc3 Marshal Zero — release qualification reset
02114b57 jjb:1011-80a92941:₢A_AAP:n: Refresh reliquary across all 13 vessels under r260426100632 (newly inscribed via tt/rbw-dI). Surfaced during AAP T1 service-suite run: four-mode conjure_lifecycle and bind_lifecycle failed at zrbfd_preflight_reliquary's HEAD on `<prefix>reliquaries/r260425082412/skopeo:r260425082412` — the old reliquary's skopeo package exists but lacks the stamp tag (rbw-irr's exists-yes is package-level not tag-level, a soft mismatch worth a separate look). Both Cloud Build modes failed identically; both local modes passed. Triage: not an AAU-passthrough bug — precheck fired before any AAU code path ran. Real cause was depot-state drift: r260425082412 partially-jettisoned (skopeo tag missing), r260327172456 never inscribed at all (referenced by 4 vessels but absent from rbxc-reliquaries/). Fix: inscribed fresh r260426100632 (Cloud Build SUCCESS at 1m16s; rbw-dY validates 6/6 tools present at the new stamp). Yoked 10 conjure vessels via rbw-dY; manually re-pointed rbev-bottle-plantuml (mode=bind) and rbev-graft-demo (mode=graft) — yoke refuses non-conjure vessels but rbfd_mirror line 1384 and rbfd_graft's about/vouch step images both consume the reliquary, so the 'no reliquary needed' policy is itself the defect (already flagged in ₢A_AAS commit b602b7c5 as follow-up; not yet captured as a formal itch — capture pending end-of-pace). Verification re-run held until user signal.
f973810d jjb:1011-dfcf5df5:₢A6AA0:n: Restructure ccyolo vessel into build-context/workspace split, replace Docker named volume with host bind mount, add system-wide UID/GID alignment for bind-mount ownership, clean entrypoint and Dockerfile of baked-in sample content, update handbook prose for workspace visibility
ce8b8648 jjb:1011-e6365e18:₢A6AAD:n: Add RBRV_USER vessel regime parameter: single source of truth for container runtime user, consumed by compose fragments, SSH, and rack. Eliminates hardcoded usernames from compose.yml and rbob_ssh.
af29ddb9 jjb:1011-39b84b28:₢A6AAG:n: Create ccyolo vessel (node:22-slim, SSH, Claude Code via npm) and nameplate (entry 2222→22, Anthropic DNS/CIDR allowlist, 10.242.3.0/24), retire CCCK

content.text:
Yes — for the regime-validation failures, source confirmed: marshal-zero state. Commit 972e1dc3 ("Marshal Zero — release qualification reset") landed Apr 27 10:24 (before my BBAAN work started at 10:31) and zeroed every vessel's `RBRV_RELIQUARY=` and every nameplate's `RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK=`. The two `rv-positive` tests walk all vessels/nameplates and call `zrbrv_enforce`/`zrbrn_enforce`, which require those fields populated — they fail by construction in pristine state.

My BBAAN commits touched only 4 files: rbgg_Governor.sh, rbgg_cli.sh, rbyc_common.sh, rbz_zipper.sh. None of the rbev-vessels or .rbk nameplate files. So these aren't BBAAN regressions.

The regime-smoke 4 failures (rbrr, rbrn, rbrv, rbrp) just appeared — let me confirm whether they're the same marshal-zero artifact.

content.text:
**Updated diagnosis — split source.** The smoke failures ARE mine.

- **regime-validation `rv_rbrv_all_vessels` + `rv_rbrn_all_nameplates`**: pre-existing marshal-zero state. Not BBAAN.
- **regime-smoke `rs_rbrr/rs_rbrn/rs_rbrv/rs_rbrp`**: caused by my Step C. `buz_healthcheck: no tabtarget for colophon 'rbw-arI' in tt/`. The healthcheck fires on every regime CLI's furnish chain and validates every enrolled colophon has a matching tabtarget on disk. I added six RBZ enrollments in Step C but the matching tabtargets land in Step D — Step C alone is structurally inconsistent.

The docket's "gate after B and C structurally land" implicitly assumed C could be a green checkpoint independent of D. It can't, given the healthcheck. The gate did its job — it caught the half-landed state.

**Path forward:** proceed to Step D (which will create the six tabtargets), then re-run gate. That will be the real green checkpoint.

params.intent: Step D of cult-verb-foundation: tabtarget swap. Delete the four old SA-management tabtargets (rbw-aC GovernorChartersRetriever, rbw-aF GovernorForfeitsServiceAccount, rbw-aK GovernorKnightsDirector, rbw-aL GovernorListsServiceAccounts) and create six new tabtargets matching the cult-verb colophon scheme: rbw-arI GovernorInvestsRetriever, rbw-adI GovernorInvestsDirector, rbw-arD GovernorDivestsRetriever, rbw-adD GovernorDivestsDirector, rbw-arr GovernorRostersRetrievers, rbw-adr GovernorRostersDirectors. Frontispieces follow the existing subject-verb-object precedent (GovernorChartersRetriever, GovernorListsServiceAccounts) with the governor cast as the actor and the role as direct object; roster forms pluralize the role per the function-shape pluralization established in Step C's RBZ constants (RBZ_ROSTER_RETRIEVERS / RBZ_ROSTER_DIRECTORS). Tabtarget bodies are the standard three-line BUW launcher boilerplate (BURD_LAUNCHER='.buk/launcher.rbw_workbench.sh', exec to launcher, identity preserved across colophons by ${0##*/}); the colophon and frontispiece live entirely in the filename per the BUK tabtarget convention. RBW dispatch routes by colophon prefix (rbw-arI matches RBZ_INVEST_RETRIEVER) so the runtime wiring closes through Step C's enrollments. RBW_aM PayorMantlesGovernor untouched per docket: governor lifecycle stays singleton-per-depot. Mid-pace structural sanity check: regime-smoke (rbtdrf_rs_rbrr / rbrp / rbrn / rbrv) was failing after Step C with 'buz_healthcheck: no tabtarget for colophon rbw-arI in tt/' — the healthcheck fires at every regime CLI's furnish-time module-kindle and validates every enrolled colophon has a matching tabtarget on disk, so Step C's enrollments-without-tabtargets state was structurally inconsistent across the whole regime-render surface (not just the rbgg surface). Step D closes the inconsistency. Re-running rbtd-r.Run.regime-smoke.sh after this commit shows rs_rbrr / rs_rbrp move from FAILED to PASSED; rs_rbrn / rs_rbrv still fail but the failure-mode flips to 'RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK must not be empty' / 'RBRV_RELIQUARY must not be empty' — these are the marshal-zero workspace-state failures (commit 972e1dc3 Apr 27 10:24, before BBAAN started) that ALSO surface as the rv-positive rbrv_all_vessels / rbrn_all_nameplates failures in regime-validation. Pre-existing, not BBAAN-caused; depot-scoped fields are blank-by-design in marshal-zero state, and the rv-positive 'all_vessels' / 'all_nameplates' tests plus rs_rbrn / rs_rbrv smoke tests assume populated state that contradicts marshal-zero. The discoverability is exactly what ₣BB heat exists to address (silent-first-build path test gap; rbw-tP qualification tier should be the gate for marshal-zero verification, not the existing fast suite). bash -n n/a (3-line launcher boilerplate, identical to existing tabtarget shape). Pace state: function surface, enrollments, and tabtargets all consistent; depot-unmake fold (Step E) and tabtarget context regen (Step F) follow.

rbgp_depot_unmake() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "DANGER: Permanently destroy an entire depot infrastructure"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step 'Safety confirmation required'
  buc_require "DANGER: Permanently destroy depot ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID} and ALL resources" "${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"

  buc_step 'Authenticate as Payor'
  local z_token
  z_token=$(zrbgp_authenticate_capture) || buc_die "Failed to authenticate as Payor via OAuth"

  buc_step 'Validate target depot'
  local -r z_project_info_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V3}/projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"
  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_project_info_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_destroy_validate"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "Validate depot project" "depot_destroy_validate"
  
  local z_lifecycle_state
  z_lifecycle_state=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_destroy_validate" '.state // "UNKNOWN"') || buc_die "Failed to parse project state"
  
  if test "${z_lifecycle_state}" != "ACTIVE"; then
    if test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "DELETE_REQUESTED"; then
      buc_die "Project already marked for deletion"
    else
      buc_die "Project state is ${z_lifecycle_state} - can only destroy ACTIVE projects"
    fi
  fi

  buc_step 'Check for and remove liens'
  local -r z_liens_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/liens?parent=projects%2F${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"
  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_liens_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_destroy_liens_list"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "List liens" "depot_destroy_liens_list"
  
  local z_lien_count
  z_lien_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_destroy_liens_list" '.liens // [] | length') || buc_die "Failed to parse liens response"
  
  if test "${z_lien_count}" -gt 0; then
    buc_log_args "Found ${z_lien_count} lien(s) - removing them"
    local z_lien_names
    z_lien_names=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_destroy_liens_list" '.liens[].name' | tr '\n' ' ') || buc_die "Failed to extract lien names"
    
    for z_lien_name in ${z_lien_names}; do
      if test -n "${z_lien_name}"; then
        buc_log_args "Removing lien: ${z_lien_name}"
        local z_delete_lien_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/liens/${z_lien_name}"
        rbgu_http_json "DELETE" "${z_delete_lien_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_destroy_lien_delete"
        rbgu_http_require_ok "Delete lien" "depot_destroy_lien_delete"
      fi
    done
  fi

  buc_step 'Unlink billing account (releases quota immediately)'
  local -r z_billing_unlink_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_billing_unlink.json"
  echo '{"billingAccountName":""}' > "${z_billing_unlink_body}" || buc_die "Failed to build billing unlink body"

  local -r z_billing_unlink_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBILLING}${RBGC_CLOUDBILLING_V1}/projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/billingInfo"
  rbgu_http_json "PUT" "${z_billing_unlink_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_destroy_billing_unlink" "${z_billing_unlink_body}"

  local z_billing_unlink_code
  z_billing_unlink_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "depot_destroy_billing_unlink") || z_billing_unlink_code=""
  if test "${z_billing_unlink_code}" = "200"; then
    buc_log_args "Billing account unlinked - quota released"
  else
    buc_warn "Could not unlink billing (HTTP ${z_billing_unlink_code}) - proceeding with deletion anyway"
  fi

  buc_step 'Delete dual worker pools (if exist)'
  # Delete tether pool
  local -r z_tether_del_id="${RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM}${RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_TETHER}"
  local -r z_tether_del_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBUILD}${RBGC_CLOUDBUILD_V1}/projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBRR_GCP_REGION}${RBGC_PATH_WORKER_POOLS}/${z_tether_del_id}"
  rbgu_http_json "DELETE" "${z_tether_del_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_destroy_pool_tether"
  local z_tether_del_code
  z_tether_del_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "depot_destroy_pool_tether") || z_tether_del_code=""
  case "${z_tether_del_code}" in
    200|204|404) buc_log_args "Tether pool ${z_tether_del_id} cleanup: HTTP ${z_tether_del_code}" ;;
    *) buc_warn "Tether pool cleanup failed: HTTP ${z_tether_del_code} — proceeding" ;;
  esac

  # Delete airgap pool
  local -r z_airgap_del_id="${RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM}${RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_AIRGAP}"
  local -r z_airgap_del_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBUILD}${RBGC_CLOUDBUILD_V1}/projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBRR_GCP_REGION}${RBGC_PATH_WORKER_POOLS}/${z_airgap_del_id}"
  rbgu_http_json "DELETE" "${z_airgap_del_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_destroy_pool_airgap"
  local z_airgap_del_code
  z_airgap_del_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "depot_destroy_pool_airgap") || z_airgap_del_code=""
  case "${z_airgap_del_code}" in
    200|204|404) buc_log_args "Airgap pool ${z_airgap_del_id} cleanup: HTTP ${z_airgap_del_code}" ;;
    *) buc_warn "Airgap pool cleanup failed: HTTP ${z_airgap_del_code} — proceeding" ;;
  esac

  buc_step 'Initiate depot deletion'
  local -r z_delete_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V3}/projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"
  rbgu_http_json "DELETE" "${z_delete_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_destroy_delete"
  
  local z_delete_response
  z_delete_response=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "depot_destroy_delete") || buc_die "Failed to get deletion response code"
  
  if test "${z_delete_response}" = "200" || test "${z_delete_response}" = "204"; then
    buc_log_args "Project deletion initiated successfully"
  else
    local z_error_msg
    z_error_msg=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_destroy_delete" '.error.message // "Unknown error"') || z_error_msg="HTTP ${z_delete_response}"
    buc_die "Failed to initiate project deletion: ${z_error_msg}"
  fi

  buc_step 'Verify deletion state transition'
  local -r z_max_attempts=12
  local z_attempt=1
  local z_final_state
  
  while test "${z_attempt}" -le "${z_max_attempts}"; do
    sleep 5
    buc_log_args "Checking deletion state (attempt ${z_attempt}/${z_max_attempts})"
    
    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_project_info_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_destroy_state_check"

    local z_state_check_code
    z_state_check_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "depot_destroy_state_check") || z_state_check_code=""
    if test "${z_state_check_code}" = "200"; then
      z_final_state=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_destroy_state_check" '.state // "UNKNOWN"') || z_final_state="UNKNOWN"

      if test "${z_final_state}" = "DELETE_REQUESTED"; then
        break
      fi
    fi
    
    z_attempt=$((z_attempt + 1))
  done
  
  if test "${z_final_state}" != "DELETE_REQUESTED"; then

buc_step 'Validate depot project exists and is active'
  local -r z_project_info_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V3}/projects/${z_depot_project_id}"
  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_project_info_url}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGP_INFIX_PROJECT_INFO}"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "Validate depot project" "${ZRBGP_INFIX_PROJECT_INFO}"

  local z_lifecycle_state
  z_lifecycle_state=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${ZRBGP_INFIX_PROJECT_INFO}" '.state') || buc_die "Failed to get project state"
  test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "ACTIVE" || buc_die "Depot project is not ACTIVE (state: ${z_lifecycle_state})"

  test "${z_depot_project_id}" != "${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID}" || buc_die "Cannot create Governor in Payor project"

  buc_step 'List existing service accounts in depot'
  local -r z_sa_list_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/${z_depot_project_id}/serviceAccounts"
  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_sa_list_url}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGP_INFIX_GOV_LIST_SA}"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "List service accounts" "${ZRBGP_INFIX_GOV_LIST_SA}"

  buc_step 'Find and delete existing governor-* service accounts'
  local z_deleted_count=0
  local z_governor_emails
  jq -r '.accounts[]? | select(.email | startswith("governor-")) | .email' \
    "${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${ZRBGP_INFIX_GOV_LIST_SA}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}" \
    > "${ZRBGP_SCRATCH_FILE}" 2>/dev/null || true
  z_governor_emails=$(<"${ZRBGP_SCRATCH_FILE}")

  if test -n "${z_governor_emails}"; then
    local z_email
    while IFS= read -r z_email; do
      test -n "${z_email}" || continue
      buc_log_args "Deleting existing governor SA: ${z_email}"

      local -r z_delete_url="${z_sa_list_url}/${z_email}"
      rbgu_http_json "DELETE" "${z_delete_url}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGP_INFIX_GOV_DELETE_SA}"

      local z_delete_code
      z_delete_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${ZRBGP_INFIX_GOV_DELETE_SA}") || z_delete_code=""
      case "${z_delete_code}" in
        200|204) z_deleted_count=$((z_deleted_count + 1)) ;;
        404)     buc_log_args "SA already deleted: ${z_email}" ;;
        *)       buc_warn "Failed to delete SA ${z_email}: HTTP ${z_delete_code}" ;;
      esac
    done <<< "${z_governor_emails}"
  fi

  buc_info "Deleted ${z_deleted_count} existing governor service account(s)"

  buc_step 'Generate Governor timestamp and account ID'
  local z_timestamp
  date +%Y%m%d%H%M > "${ZRBGP_SCRATCH_FILE}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to generate timestamp"
  z_timestamp=$(<"${ZRBGP_SCRATCH_FILE}")

done
  
  if test "${z_final_state}" != "DELETE_REQUESTED"; then
    buc_die "Failed to verify deletion state transition. Current state: ${z_final_state}"
  fi

  buc_step 'Update depot tracking'
  zrbgp_depot_list_update || buc_log_args "Warning: Failed to update depot tracking after deletion"

  # Success
  buc_success "Depot ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID} successfully marked for deletion"
  buc_info "Project Status: DELETE_REQUESTED"
  buc_info "Billing: Unlinked (quota released immediately)"
  buc_info "Grace period: Up to 30 days before permanent removal"
  buc_info "All infrastructure (Mason SA, repository, bucket) will be automatically removed"
}

rbgp_depot_list() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "List all depot instances and their status"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step 'Authenticate as Payor'
  local z_token
  z_token=$(zrbgp_authenticate_capture) || buc_die "Failed to authenticate as Payor via OAuth"

  buc_step 'Probe depot states and emit per-moniker fact files'
  zrbgp_depot_state_emit "${z_token}"

  buc_step 'Render depot summary'
  local z_total_count=0
  local z_complete_count=0
  local z_delete_requested_count=0

  buc_info ""
  buc_info "=== DEPOT SUMMARY ==="
  printf "%-40s %s\n" "PROJECT_ID" "STATUS"

  # Per-iteration synthesized locals (BCG exception 2)
  local z_fact_path=""
  local z_basename=""
  local z_moniker=""
  local z_project_id=""
  local z_project_fact_path=""
  local z_state=""

  # Walk emitted depot fact files; stem is the moniker, content is the state.
  # The sidecar depot-project fact file carries the canonical project_id.
  shopt -s nullglob
  for z_fact_path in "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}"/*.${RBCC_fact_ext_depot}; do
    z_basename="${z_fact_path##*/}"
    z_moniker="${z_basename%.${RBCC_fact_ext_depot}}"
    z_project_fact_path="${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${z_moniker}.${RBCC_fact_ext_depot_project}"
    z_state=$(<"${z_fact_path}")
    test -n "${z_state}" || buc_die "Empty state in fact file: ${z_fact_path}"
    test -f "${z_project_fact_path}" || buc_die "Missing depot-project fact file: ${z_project_fact_path}"
    z_project_id=$(<"${z_project_fact_path}")
    test -n "${z_project_id}" || buc_die "Empty project_id in fact file: ${z_project_fact_path}"

    printf "%-40s %s\n" "${z_project_id}" "${z_state}"
    z_total_count=$((z_total_count + 1))
    case "${z_state}" in
      "${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE}")          z_complete_count=$((z_complete_count + 1)) ;;
      "${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED}")  z_delete_requested_count=$((z_delete_requested_count + 1)) ;;
    esac
  done
  shopt -u nullglob

  if test "${z_total_count}" -eq 0; then
    buc_info "No depot projects found"
    return 0
  fi

  buc_info ""
  buc_info "=== SUMMARY ==="
  buc_info "Total depots:     ${z_total_count}"
  buc_info "Complete:         ${z_complete_count}"
  buc_info "Delete-requested: ${z_delete_requested_count}"
}

rbgp_payor_oauth_refresh() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Refresh expired OAuth credentials following RBAGS manual procedure"
  buc_doc_lines "Use this when OAuth tokens expire after 6 months or are compromised"
  buc_doc_lines "Requires downloading new OAuth JSON from Google Cloud Console"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step 'Display OAuth refresh procedure'
  buc_info ""
  buc_info "=== Manual Payor OAuth Refresh Procedure ==="
  buc_info ""
  buc_info "OAuth credentials need to be refreshed. Follow these steps:"
  buc_info ""
  buc_info "1. Navigate to APIs & Services > Credentials in Payor Project"
  buc_info "   Console URL: https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials?project=${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID}"
  buc_info ""
  buc_info "2. Find existing 'Recipe Bottle Payor' OAuth client"
  buc_info ""  
  buc_info "3. Download new JSON credentials:"
  buc_info "   - Click the download icon next to the OAuth client"
  buc_info "   - Or regenerate client secret if compromised"
  buc_info ""
  date +%Y%m%d > "${ZRBGP_SCRATCH_FILE}" 2>/dev/null || true
  local z_today_stamp=$(<"${ZRBGP_SCRATCH_FILE}")
  buc_info "4. Save as timestamped file:"
  buc_info "   - Example: payor-oauth-${z_today_stamp}.json"
  buc_info ""
  buc_info "5. Run installation command with new JSON:"
  buc_info "   rbgp_payor_install /path/to/payor-oauth-[timestamp].json"
  buc_info ""
  buc_info "This will regenerate OAuth credentials and update RBRO file."
  buc_info ""
  
  buc_success "OAuth refresh procedure displayed"
  buc_info "Note: OAuth refresh tokens expire after 6 months of non-use in testing mode"
  buc_info "Any successful payor operation resets the 6-month timer"
}

rbgp_governor_mantle() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  local -r z_depot_project_id="${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"

  buc_doc_brief "Create or replace Governor service account in a depot"
  buc_doc_lines "This operation is idempotent: existing governor-* SAs are deleted before creating a new one"
  buc_doc_lines "Targets depot derived from RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX + RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER (RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

buc_step 'Validate target depot'
  local -r z_project_info_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V3}/projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"
  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_project_info_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_destroy_validate"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "Validate depot project" "depot_destroy_validate"
  
  local z_lifecycle_state
  z_lifecycle_state=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_destroy_validate" '.state // "UNKNOWN"') || buc_die "Failed to parse project state"
  
  if test "${z_lifecycle_state}" != "ACTIVE"; then
    if test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "DELETE_REQUESTED"; then
      buc_die "Project already marked for deletion"
    else
      buc_die "Project state is ${z_lifecycle_state} - can only destroy ACTIVE projects"
    fi
  fi

  buc_step 'Check for and remove liens'
  local -r z_liens_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/liens?parent=projects%2F${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"
  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_liens_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_destroy_liens_list"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "List liens" "depot_destroy_liens_list"

input.new_string:
if test "${z_lifecycle_state}" != "ACTIVE"; then
    if test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "DELETE_REQUESTED"; then
      buc_die "Project already marked for deletion"
    else
      buc_die "Project state is ${z_lifecycle_state} - can only destroy ACTIVE projects"
    fi
  fi

  buc_step 'Clean up governor service accounts (404-tolerant)'
  # No standalone demantle verb — governor SA lifecycle ends here.  Project
  # deletion would clean these up implicitly, but an explicit DELETE pass
  # gives diagnostic visibility and makes the cleanup contract testable.
  local -r z_unmake_sa_url_base="${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/serviceAccounts"
  local z_unmake_sa_page_token=""
  local z_unmake_sa_page=1
  local z_unmake_sa_url=""
  local z_unmake_sa_tok_enc=""
  local z_unmake_sa_infix=""
  local z_unmake_sa_count=0
  local z_unmake_sa_index=0
  local z_unmake_sa_email=""
  local z_unmake_gov_delete_infix=""
  local z_governor_sa_count=0
  while :; do
    z_unmake_sa_url="${z_unmake_sa_url_base}"
    if test -n "${z_unmake_sa_page_token}"; then
      z_unmake_sa_tok_enc=$(rbgu_urlencode_capture "${z_unmake_sa_page_token}") \
        || buc_die "Failed to URL-encode pageToken"
      z_unmake_sa_url="${z_unmake_sa_url}?pageToken=${z_unmake_sa_tok_enc}"
    fi
    z_unmake_sa_infix="depot_unmake_gov_list_${z_unmake_sa_page}"
    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_unmake_sa_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_unmake_sa_infix}"
    rbgu_http_require_ok "List service accounts (page ${z_unmake_sa_page})" "${z_unmake_sa_infix}"

    z_unmake_sa_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_unmake_sa_infix}" '.accounts // [] | length') \
      || buc_die "Failed to parse SA list"

    z_unmake_sa_index=0
    while test "${z_unmake_sa_index}" -lt "${z_unmake_sa_count}"; do
      z_unmake_sa_email=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_unmake_sa_infix}" ".accounts[${z_unmake_sa_index}].email") \
        || { z_unmake_sa_index=$((z_unmake_sa_index + 1)); continue; }
      if [[ "${z_unmake_sa_email}" == ${RBCC_role_governor}-* ]]; then
        buc_log_args "Deleting governor SA: ${z_unmake_sa_email}"
        z_unmake_gov_delete_infix="depot_unmake_gov_delete_${z_governor_sa_count}"
        rbgu_http_json "DELETE" "${z_unmake_sa_url_base}/${z_unmake_sa_email}" "${z_token}" "${z_unmake_gov_delete_infix}"
        rbgu_http_require_ok "Delete governor SA ${z_unmake_sa_email}" "${z_unmake_gov_delete_infix}" \
          404 "not found (already deleted)"
        z_governor_sa_count=$((z_governor_sa_count + 1))
      fi
      z_unmake_sa_index=$((z_unmake_sa_index + 1))
    done

    z_unmake_sa_page_token=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_unmake_sa_infix}" '.nextPageToken') \
      || z_unmake_sa_page_token=""
    test -n "${z_unmake_sa_page_token}" || break
    z_unmake_sa_page=$((z_unmake_sa_page + 1))
  done
  buc_info "Governor SA cleanup: removed ${z_governor_sa_count} account(s)"

  buc_step 'Check for and remove liens'

params.intent: Step E of cult-verb-foundation: fold governor SA cleanup into rbgp_depot_unmake. Insert a new 'Clean up governor service accounts (404-tolerant)' step after the depot validation block (line 990) and before the lien check, replacing the implicit project-deletion-cleans-everything posture with an explicit DELETE pass. Implementation: paginated walk of the depot project's service-account list (RBGD/RBGC IAM v1 endpoint), per-page rbgu_http_json + rbgu_http_require_ok, per-account-index rbgu_json_field_capture extraction (BCG-compliant — no $(jq ...) direct calls), filter by glob match against ${RBCC_role_governor}-* (literal 'governor-' resolved via the centralized role tinder constant), DELETE each match with rbgu_http_require_ok '...' '...' 404 'not found (already deleted)' for the 404-tolerance pattern the docket specifies, accumulate per-delete infix-count for unique trace files, summary buc_info reports the cleanup count. Helper-function extraction (Option C: shared helper between mantle's pre-cleanup and depot_unmake's terminal cleanup) considered and rejected at mount time — mantle's pre-cleanup (rbgp_Payor.sh:1247-1270) is structurally coupled to its surrounding flow (it precedes governor regen, uses different scratch/infix discipline, and uses the same BCG-non-compliant `jq -r ... | head -N` pattern as line 341 of rbgg_Governor.sh that I surfaced for separate scope in Step B), and refactoring it would extend BBAAN scope into a tangentially-related cleanup. Inline approach is simpler, BCG-compliant, and orthogonal to mantle's existing pattern. The success-line at line 1100 ('All infrastructure (Mason SA, repository, bucket) will be automatically removed') was left as-is since the explicit governor cleanup runs BEFORE project deletion; project deletion still handles Mason / repository / bucket / etc. via implicit cleanup. Why before lien removal: governor SA cleanup needs the project ACTIVE state for the IAM list/delete API calls; lien removal and billing unlink are subsequent prep-for-project-delete operations, so SA cleanup belongs first in the prep sequence. Why before billing unlink: billing-unlink is the first irreversible step (releases quota immediately), so any cleanup that needs the project quota-active should run earlier. No-demantle-verb posture preserved: there is no rbgg_demantle / equivalent function; depot_unmake is the sole governor-SA-cleanup consumer. bash -n clean. Regime-smoke regression check after this commit: rs_rbrr / rs_rbrr_nonempty_prefix / rs_rbrp / rs_burc / rs_burs / rs_burd all PASS; rs_rbrn / rs_rbrv still fail with 'RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK / RBRV_RELIQUARY must not be empty' — same marshal-zero workspace-state failure carried forward from earlier (commit 972e1dc3, pre-BBAAN), unrelated to depot_unmake. Step F regenerates the tabtarget context next.

content.text:
All six steps complete. Final state: 7 commits on BBAAN total (3 foundation + 6 from this session). Build green; fast suite has 4 marshal-zero workspace-state failures (rv_rbrv_all_vessels, rv_rbrn_all_nameplates, rs_rbrn, rs_rbrv) that are pre-existing artifacts of commit 972e1dc3 — not BBAAN regressions.

**Uncomfortable choices to surface:**

1. **Removed `rm -f` of decoded key JSON** in zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key. Old code deleted the temp credential file at end-of-function (a credential-hygiene gesture). When I relocated the file into the rbgu cache for BCG-compliant capture, BCG:518 forbids module-level temp file deletion. So I dropped the rm. Net: the decoded private key now persists in `${ZRBGU_PREFIX}*_decoded_u_resp.json` until infrastructure cleanup. The b64 form was already persisting in the same cache (the keys.create response file), so this isn't new exposure — but it IS a behavior change in the credential-hygiene gesture.

2. **assay-slot reuse fragility.** Multiple invests overwrite the same `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE`. If an operator runs `rbw-arI alice` then `rbw-arI bob` without copying alice's bytes to her production slot in between, alice's bytes are lost. The post-invest message reminds the operator, but the workflow is fragile if operators batch operations.

3. **Mantle's BCG-non-compliant pre-cleanup left in place.** Step E added a fresh BCG-compliant governor-cleanup loop in depot_unmake. Mantle's existing pre-cleanup at rbgp_Payor.sh:1247-1270 uses the same `jq -r ... | head` direct-`$()` pattern that BCG:495 forbids. I considered extracting a shared helper but rejected as scope creep; mantle still drifts.

4. **Collapsed two-fork operator instruction to single.** Old charter/knight printed both `cp ... ${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}` and `Or, for assay (test) mode: cp ... ${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE}`. With assay-as-binding-producer-output, the source IS already in assay; only the role-production copy makes sense. Docket said "Install: cp ${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE} ${RBDC_<role>_RBRA_FILE}" — singular — so I'm faithful; just flagging the operator-message simplification.

5. **Split Step C and D mid-pace.** Step C alone is structurally inconsistent (RBZ enrollments without matching tabtargets → buz_healthcheck fails on every regime CLI). I notched C anyway and resolved in D. Per-step granularity wins, but C-alone is not a green checkpoint by itself.

6. **Roster regex uses `.+` for identity capture** rather than the strict `[a-z][a-z0-9-]*` GCP SA local-part shape. Cheap, correct for the filter intent (anything that matched `role-` prefix is in our domain), but laxer than the depot-state-emit mason regex precedent which uses `[a-z][a-z0-9]*`.

7. **invest preexistence relies on 409-fatal kindle posture** — implicit, no explicit pre-check. If the global 409-fatal posture is ever softened, invest preexistence behavior changes silently. Matches old charter_retriever's behavior exactly.

Want me to address any of these now, or accept them?

message.content:
1) I would be significantly happier if we kept all secrets touched in the assay subdirectory.  Is that a sensible fix?  
2) Operators shall never batch role creations, I decree it.  Expressly embraced.  Suggest RBS0 specification and key premise statements to get comfortable with this.
3) Lets intend to fix this before wrapping this pace, either with a repare or a newly slated pace.
4) I think I don't understand: if we write directly to 'assay' place, why do we need to copy?  Or are you confusing the advice to install the RBRA to the right place, which is just a printout of a copy command?
5) Help me remember scope of our current pace: should this work before we do final testin for this pace?  Or do we need to slate/reslate another test pace to check this issue once we have a well formed RBRR?
6) ok
7) ok

content.text:
**1) Yes, sensible — for the part I introduced.** I can:
- Write the decoded key JSON to `${RBRR_SECRETS_DIR}/${RBCC_role_assay}/_decoded_key.json` instead of the rbgu cache
- Add a small `zrbgg_secrets_json_field_capture` helper for arbitrary-path JSON field extraction (BCG-sanctioned `_capture` suffix; same shape as `rbgu_json_field_capture` but path-driven)
- `rm -f` the decoded JSON after RBRA write — assay dir is `RBRR_SECRETS_DIR`, NOT `BURD_TEMP_DIR`, so BCG:518's "no module-level temp deletion" doesn't apply

**Honest caveat:** the b64-encoded form (Google's keys.create response) ALWAYS lands in the rbgu cache at `${ZRBGU_PREFIX}<infix>_u_resp.json` because `rbgu_http_json`'s caching contract puts every response there. Relocating that requires either a wider rbgu refactor (configurable cache target) or a BCG:518 carve-out for credential-grade response files. The b64 form is one layer of encoding from being readable. I'd address only the decoded form here and surface the b64 concern as separate scope.

**2) Premise language suggestion.** The constraint is operator-workflow load-bearing. Two homes that earn it:

- **RBS0-SpecTop.adoc** — premise/axiom block (or wherever invariants live): something like

  > **Invest is serialized.** Invest writes credential bytes to a singleton assay slot, not a per-identity slot. The operator must copy `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` to the role's production slot before initiating the next invest. Two consecutive invests against the same role without an intervening copy lose the first invest's bytes. Batched invest is not supported — by design.

- **Per-quoin definitions BBAAQ will land** for `:rbgg_invest_retriever:` and `:rbgg_invest_director:` — each could carry a "Workflow constraint" line referencing the RBS0 premise. Spec sweep is BBAAQ; the premise itself can land in BBAAN if you want it source-of-truth before BBAAQ writes the consumer quoins, OR BBAAQ owns the whole thing.

I'd recommend BBAAN lands the premise in RBS0 (small, focused, no quoin authoring) and BBAAQ references it. Or it's all BBAAQ. Your call.

**3) Slate suggestion.** I propose a small focused sibling pace — let's call it **BBAAW spec-ext-or-bcg-sweep** (or whatever coronet's next):
- Site 1: `rbgp_Payor.sh:1247-1270` — mantle's `governor-*` pre-cleanup uses `jq -r ... | tr ...` and `${z_governor_emails}<<<` direct shell. Refactor to paginated rbgu_json_field_capture pattern (mirror Step E's depot_unmake cleanup loop).
- Site 2: `rbgg_Governor.sh:341` — `zrbgg_list_bucket_objects_capture`'s pageToken extraction uses `$(jq -r ...)` directly. Flip to rbgu_json_field_capture.

Both same fix kind, both BCG:495 violations, both mechanical. Could also land inline as a Step G in BBAAN if you'd rather bundle. Light preference for sibling pace because BBAAN's docket scope was cult-verb-foundation; mixing in a BCG drift sweep dilutes that. Either works.

**4) Yes, you understand correctly — I was over-flagging.** The printout is just an operator-instruction `cp` command. The flow is:
- `rbw-arI alice` writes RBRA bytes to `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` (assay = singleton producer output)
- Operator copies `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` → `RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE` (role production slot, where runtime reads)

The two-step shape is intentional (assay = uniform producer output, role-prod = decided promotion). The old charter/knight printed BOTH `cp ... role-prod` AND `Or, for assay (test) mode: cp ... assay`. The second one was nonsense post-design-collapse since the bytes ALREADY START in assay. I dropped it. Net: one `cp` instruction, no fork. Faithful to docket. Not actually uncomfortable now that I think about it more.

**5) Mantle BCG fix does NOT need to land before BBAAN's final testing.** BBAAN's scope is cult-verb-foundation; the mantle pre-cleanup pattern works correctly (just code-quality-non-compliant), and final testing for BBAAN should exercise the invest/divest/roster surface end-to-end against a real depot — which requires populated RBRR (post-marshal-zero state). That end-to-end testing happens whenever you next provision a real depot, OR via a service-suite run, OR as part of pristine-tier qualification. The mantle BCG fix is orthogonal to that exercise and can land in a sibling pace (or inline pre-wrap) without coupling.

So: BBAAN testing happens later (post-RBRR-population); mantle BCG fix is independent.

**Recommended next moves** (in priority order):
1. (1) Relocate decoded-key bytes to assay subdir + small helper. Small fix, fits BBAAN.
2. (2) Land the "invest is serialized" premise in RBS0. Small fix, fits BBAAN.
3. (3) Slate sibling pace for the BCG drift sweep (or do inline as Step G). Your call.

Want me to do all three? Or pick a subset?

zrbgg_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBGG_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbgg not kindled - call zrbgg_kindle first"

# Path-driven sibling of rbgu_json_field_capture for files that live outside

# must not leak into BURD_TEMP_DIR).
zrbgg_secrets_json_field_capture() {
  zrbgg_sentinel
  local -r z_path="${1}"
  local -r z_jq="${2}"
  local z_result
  z_result=$(jq -r "${z_jq}" "${z_path}")  || return 1
  if test -z "${z_result}" || test "${z_result}" = "null"; then return 1; fi
  echo "${z_result}"
}

input.old_string:
buc_step 'Extract and decode key data'
  local z_key_b64
  z_key_b64=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_key_infix}" '.privateKeyData') \
    || buc_die "Failed to extract privateKeyData"
  # Decode into the rbgu cache so rbgu_json_field_capture (BCG-sanctioned _capture)
  # can address the decoded key JSON by infix.
  local z_key_decoded_infix="${z_key_infix}_decoded"
  local z_key_json="${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${z_key_decoded_infix}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}"
  printf '%s' "${z_key_b64}" | openssl enc -base64 -d -A > "${z_key_json}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to decode key data"

  buc_step 'Convert JSON key to RBRA format'
  local z_rbra_file="${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE}"

  local z_client_email
  z_client_email=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_key_decoded_infix}" '.client_email') \
    || buc_die "Failed to extract client_email from key JSON"

  local z_private_key
  z_private_key=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_key_decoded_infix}" '.private_key') \
    || buc_die "Failed to extract private_key from key JSON"

  local z_project_id
  z_project_id=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_key_decoded_infix}" '.project_id') \
    || buc_die "Failed to extract project_id from key JSON"

  buc_step 'Write RBRA file' "${z_rbra_file}"
  {
    printf 'RBRA_ROLE=%s\n'                  "${z_role}"
    printf 'RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL="%s"\n'      "$z_client_email"
    printf 'RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY="'; printf '%s' "$z_private_key"; printf '"\n'
    printf 'RBRA_PROJECT_ID="%s"\n'        "$z_project_id"
    printf 'RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC=1800\n'
  } > "${z_rbra_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write RBRA file ${z_rbra_file}"

  test -f "${z_rbra_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write RBRA file ${z_rbra_file}"

  buc_info "RBRA file written: ${z_rbra_file}"
input.new_string:
buc_step 'Extract and decode key data'
  local z_key_b64
  z_key_b64=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_key_infix}" '.privateKeyData') \
    || buc_die "Failed to extract privateKeyData"
  # Decode into the assay subdirectory (RBRR_SECRETS_DIR/assay/) so the
  # only readable form of the private key shares lifecycle and location
  # with the final RBRA file — credentials never leak into BURD_TEMP_DIR.
  local -r z_assay_dir="${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE%/*}"
  local -r z_key_json="${z_assay_dir}/_decoded_key_${z_account_name}.json"
  printf '%s' "${z_key_b64}" | openssl enc -base64 -d -A > "${z_key_json}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to decode key data"

  buc_step 'Convert JSON key to RBRA format'
  local z_rbra_file="${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE}"

  local z_client_email
  z_client_email=$(zrbgg_secrets_json_field_capture "${z_key_json}" '.client_email') \
    || buc_die "Failed to extract client_email from key JSON"

  local z_private_key
  z_private_key=$(zrbgg_secrets_json_field_capture "${z_key_json}" '.private_key') \
    || buc_die "Failed to extract private_key from key JSON"

  local z_project_id
  z_project_id=$(zrbgg_secrets_json_field_capture "${z_key_json}" '.project_id') \
    || buc_die "Failed to extract project_id from key JSON"

  buc_step 'Write RBRA file' "${z_rbra_file}"
  {
    printf 'RBRA_ROLE=%s\n'                  "${z_role}"
    printf 'RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL="%s"\n'      "$z_client_email"
    printf 'RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY="'; printf '%s' "$z_private_key"; printf '"\n'
    printf 'RBRA_PROJECT_ID="%s"\n'        "$z_project_id"
    printf 'RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC=1800\n'
  } > "${z_rbra_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write RBRA file ${z_rbra_file}"

  test -f "${z_rbra_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write RBRA file ${z_rbra_file}"

  # Decoded JSON lives in RBRR_SECRETS_DIR (not BURD_TEMP_DIR), so
  # BCG:518's no-module-temp-deletion rule does not bind. Remove now
  # that the bytes are persisted in RBRA form.
  rm -f "${z_key_json}"

  buc_info "RBRA file written: ${z_rbra_file}"

777	== Key Premises
778	
779	[[rbsk_no_gcloud]]
780	//axl_voices axk_premise
781	{rbsk_no_gcloud}::
782	{at_recipe_bottle}
783	scripts MUST NOT invoke the `gcloud` CLI in local bash scripts or transitively through helper scripts.
784	All local
785	{gcp_service}
786	interaction uses direct REST API calls via `curl` with JWT-authenticated bearer tokens.
787	+
788	**Exception — vouch provenance fetch**: The {rbtgo_ark_vouch} verification step (conjure mode only) invokes `gcloud artifacts docker images describe --show-provenance` for SLSA provenance envelope retrieval.
789	This wraps complex Container Analysis API interaction that would be burdensome to replicate via raw REST.
790	All other GCB step operations use the GCE metadata server for OAuth token acquisition and direct registry REST API calls — no gcloud.
791	The provenance fetch runs as Python 3 on the gcloud builder image after the primary image is built; the reproducibility concern that motivates this premise is weaker for post-build verification.
792	Works under NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS (Private Google Access reaches Google APIs).
793	+
794	In GCB build step contexts (conjure, bind, enshrine), registry authentication uses the GCE metadata server to obtain an access token and writes a static Docker config JSON.
795	+
796	Rationale: `gcloud` is an opaque, version-sensitive binary that obscures API semantics, introduces implicit authentication state, and breaks reproducibility.
797	The `gcloud auth configure-docker` approach also fails mechanically in GCB steps — the gcloud builder image lacks `docker` in PATH, so the credential helper it installs cannot function.
798	+
799	NOTE: The gcloud reliquary image exists because conjure build steps use it as a convenience shell (for `bash` and `date`).
800	The vouch verification step (conjure mode) additionally invokes `gcloud` for provenance retrieval (see exception above).
801

file.content:
for the manual Console procedure to review build capacity settings.

== Key Premises

[[rbsk_no_gcloud]]
//axl_voices axk_premise
{rbsk_no_gcloud}::
{at_recipe_bottle}
scripts MUST NOT invoke the `gcloud` CLI in local bash scripts or transitively through helper scripts.
All local
{gcp_service}
interaction uses direct REST API calls via `curl` with JWT-authenticated bearer tokens.
+
**Exception — vouch provenance fetch**: The {rbtgo_ark_vouch} verification step (conjure mode only) invokes `gcloud artifacts docker images describe --show-provenance` for SLSA provenance envelope retrieval.
This wraps complex Container Analysis API interaction that would be burdensome to replicate via raw REST.
All other GCB step operations use the GCE metadata server for OAuth token acquisition and direct registry REST API calls — no gcloud.
The provenance fetch runs as Python 3 on the gcloud builder image after the primary image is built; the reproducibility concern that motivates this premise is weaker for post-build verification.
Works under NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS (Private Google Access reaches Google APIs).
+
In GCB build step contexts (conjure, bind, enshrine), registry authentication uses the GCE metadata server to obtain an access token and writes a static Docker config JSON.
+
Rationale: `gcloud` is an opaque, version-sensitive binary that obscures API semantics, introduces implicit authentication state, and breaks reproducibility.
The `gcloud auth configure-docker` approach also fails mechanically in GCB steps — the gcloud builder image lacks `docker` in PATH, so the credential helper it installs cannot function.
+
NOTE: The gcloud reliquary image exists because conjure build steps use it as a convenience shell (for `bash` and `date`).
The vouch verification step (conjure mode) additionally invokes `gcloud` for provenance retrieval (see exception above).

== Dependency Inventory

{at_recipe_bottle} depends on a minimal, explicitly managed set of external commands.
The Bash Console Guide (`BCG-BashConsoleGuide.md`) establishes the universal dependency discipline — POSIX builtins preferred, external commands justified individually.
This section inventories the concrete choices {at_recipe_bottle} has made within that framework.

=== Consumer Dependencies

Required on every {at_workstation}.
Enforced by qualification and documented on the project page.

[cols="1,3"]

1040	and
1041	{rbtr_director}
1042	service accounts share a cult-verb triad: invest mints the SA and emits its RBRA credential bytes, divest tears it down, and roster lists existing instances.
1043	Each verb has per-role instances; the colophon shape is `rbw-a<role-letter><verb-letter>` where the letter case carries semantic load — capital `I` (invest) and `D` (divest) for cloud-mutating verbs, lowercase `r` (roster) for read-only observation, mirroring the existing `rbw-d[lL]` precedent.
1044	{rbtgo_governor_mantle} stays as the singleton-per-depot exception; governor SA cleanup folds into {rbtgo_depot_unmake}.
