input.new_string:
NOTE: The gcloud reliquary image exists because conjure build steps use it as a convenience shell (for `bash` and `date`).
The vouch verification step (conjure mode) additionally invokes `gcloud` for provenance retrieval (see exception above).

[[rbsk_invest_serialized]]
//axl_voices axk_premise
{rbsk_invest_serialized}::
{rbtgo_retriever_invest} and {rbtgo_director_invest} write credential bytes to a singleton assay slot (`RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE`), not a per-identity slot.
The operator MUST copy `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` to the role's production slot (`RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE` or `RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE`) before initiating the next invest against the same role.
Two consecutive invests against the same role without an intervening copy lose the first invest's bytes.
Batched invest is not supported — by design.
+
Rationale: a singleton assay slot keeps the producer-output discipline uniform across roles and frees the implementation from path-space per-identity routing.
The operator-driven copy step is the deliberate promotion gate from producer output to role-production consumer; it also acts as the operator's explicit acknowledgement of which identity owns the role at runtime, since `RBRA` files encode exactly one identity at a time.

== Dependency Inventory

params.intent: Step G of cult-verb-foundation: address two operator-asks surfaced in the post-Step-F uncomfortable-choice review. (1) Relocate decoded private-key bytes out of BURD_TEMP_DIR (the rbgu cache) into the assay subdirectory so credential lifecycle and location share a tree with the final RBRA. New private helper zrbgg_secrets_json_field_capture (path-driven sibling of rbgu_json_field_capture, BCG-sanctioned via _capture suffix); decode target moves from ${ZRBGU_PREFIX}<infix>_decoded_u_resp.json to ${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE%/*}/_decoded_key_${z_account_name}.json (composing the assay role subdir from the assay rbra's parent dir, so the path tracks RBDC kindle automatically); three credential-field captures flip from rbgu_json_field_capture to zrbgg_secrets_json_field_capture; rm -f the decoded JSON immediately after RBRA write — BCG:518's no-module-temp-deletion rule scopes specifically to BURD_TEMP_DIR, and the assay subdirectory is RBRR_SECRETS_DIR territory with different (operator-managed) lifecycle, so deletion is permitted and credential-hygiene-correct. Honest caveat (deferred): the b64-encoded form (Google's keys.create response) still lands in the rbgu cache at ${ZRBGU_PREFIX}<key_infix>_u_resp.json because rbgu_http_json's caching contract puts every response there; relocating that requires either a wider rbgu cache-target refactor or a BCG:518 carve-out for credential-grade response files — separate scope, surfaced for future work. The b64 form is one layer of encoding from being readable, so this remains a transient exposure in BURD_TEMP_DIR until a scoped rbgu fix lands. (2) Land the 'Invest Serialization' premise in RBS0-SpecTop.adoc as a load-bearing operator-workflow constraint. Three-part linked term shape per MCM: attribute reference :rbsk_invest_serialized: in the Key Premises mapping section (line 503, sibling to :rbsk_no_gcloud:), [[rbsk_invest_serialized]] anchor + axl_voices axk_premise voicing + definition body in the == Key Premises section (line 803, after rbsk_no_gcloud's complete block, before == Dependency Inventory). Premise text references existing quoins {rbtgo_retriever_invest} and {rbtgo_director_invest} (already minted in RBS0); names the literal env-var paths RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE / RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE / RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE rather than minting new path quoins (BBAAQ owns spec quoin authoring). Premise asserts: invest writes to a singleton assay slot, operator MUST copy to role-production slot before next invest, two consecutive invests without intervening copy lose the first identity's bytes, batched invest is not supported by design. Rationale paragraph explains the producer-output uniformity and the operator-driven promotion-gate semantic (the copy step ALSO encodes which identity owns the role at runtime, since RBRA files single-identity by file shape). Operator's 'I decree it. Expressly embraced.' carries directly into the spec as the explicit constraint. Regime-smoke after this commit: same 2 marshal-zero workspace-state failures (rs_rbrn / rs_rbrv carrying RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK / RBRV_RELIQUARY blanks from commit 972e1dc3, pre-BBAAN), no new regressions; the rbgg_Governor.sh changes are closed-system within zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key's body and don't touch any furnish/dispatch surface. (3) Mantle BCG-sweep slate is enrolled separately as a sibling pace via jjx_enroll — the two BCG:495 violation sites (rbgp_Payor.sh:1247-1270 mantle pre-cleanup, rbgg_Governor.sh:341 zrbgg_list_bucket_objects_capture pageToken extraction) get a focused mechanical-fix pace rather than dilute BBAAN's cult-verb-foundation scope.

# jjezs_slate bcg-direct-jq-sweep

## Character

Mechanical BCG:495 fix-up across two surfaced sites where `$(jq -r ...)` direct command-substitution captures external-tool output — forbidden outside `_capture` / `_recite` functions. Pre-existing drift (predates BBAAN) surfaced during the BBAAN cult-verb-foundation pace's BCG-flag chase. Carved into a focused sibling pace to keep the BBAAN docket on cult-verb-foundation; this pace owns ONLY the BCG sweep.

## Sites

Two sites, same fix kind:

1. `Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh:1247-1270` — `rbgp_governor_mantle`'s pre-cleanup of existing `governor-*` SAs uses `jq -r '.accounts[]? | select(.email | startswith("governor-")) | .email' "${...}.json" > "${ZRBGP_SCRATCH_FILE}" 2>/dev/null || true; z_governor_emails=$(<"${ZRBGP_SCRATCH_FILE}")` then iterates with `<<<` heredoc-string. The `2>/dev/null || true` also violates BCG:520 stderr-never-suppress.

2. `Tools/rbk/rbgg_Governor.sh:341` — `zrbgg_list_bucket_objects_capture`'s pageToken extraction uses `z_page_token=$(jq -r '.nextPageToken // empty' "${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${z_infix}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}") || return 1`. Sibling `_capture` function but BCG:495 still applies to its internals.

## Fix kind

Both flip to `rbgu_json_field_capture` (BCG-sanctioned `_capture`) with appropriate jq expressions. Site 1 needs additional restructure to handle the multi-row email list — either paginated walk via `.accounts // [] | length` + per-index `.accounts[N].email` (Step E's depot_unmake cleanup is the canonical pattern in BBAAN) OR jq-output-to-temp-file then builtin read. Stderr suppression at site 1 also retires (capture stderr to a discriminated tempfile per BCG:520).

## Out of scope

- Cult-verb-foundation surface (BBAAN owns).
- Other BCG drift not at these two sites (e.g. mantle's broader structural shape, scratch-file discipline elsewhere).
- New behavior; this is mechanical correctness-preserving fix.

## Verification

- bash -n clean across both files.
- regime-smoke / regime-validation no regressions vs. pre-pace baseline (same marshal-zero failures expected, nothing new).
- Pristine-tier verification of `rbgp_governor_mantle` happens later via the BB heat's `rbw-tP` qualification pipeline; this pace doesn't gate on it.

echo OK

# jjezs_slate bcg-direct-jq-sweep

## Character

Mechanical BCG:495 fix-up across two surfaced sites where `$(jq -r ...)` direct command-substitution captures external-tool output — forbidden outside `_capture` / `_recite` functions. Pre-existing drift (predates BBAAN) surfaced during the BBAAN cult-verb-foundation pace's BCG-flag chase. Carved into a focused sibling pace to keep the BBAAN docket on cult-verb-foundation; this pace owns ONLY the BCG sweep.

## Sites

Two sites, same fix kind:

1. `Tools/rbk/rbgp_Payor.sh:1247-1270` — `rbgp_governor_mantle`'s pre-cleanup of existing `governor-*` SAs uses `jq -r '.accounts[]? | select(.email | startswith("governor-")) | .email' "${...}.json" > "${ZRBGP_SCRATCH_FILE}" 2>/dev/null || true; z_governor_emails=$(<"${ZRBGP_SCRATCH_FILE}")` then iterates with `<<<` heredoc-string. The `2>/dev/null || true` also violates BCG:520 stderr-never-suppress.

2. `Tools/rbk/rbgg_Governor.sh:341` — `zrbgg_list_bucket_objects_capture`'s pageToken extraction uses `z_page_token=$(jq -r '.nextPageToken // empty' "${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${z_infix}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}") || return 1`. Sibling `_capture` function but BCG:495 still applies to its internals.

## Fix kind

Both flip to `rbgu_json_field_capture` (BCG-sanctioned `_capture`) with appropriate jq expressions. Site 1 needs additional restructure to handle the multi-row email list — either paginated walk via `.accounts // [] | length` + per-index `.accounts[N].email` (BBAAN Step E's depot_unmake cleanup is the canonical pattern in-tree) OR jq-output-to-temp-file then builtin read. Stderr suppression at site 1 also retires (capture stderr to a discriminated tempfile per BCG:520).

## Out of scope

- Cult-verb-foundation surface (BBAAN owns).
- Other BCG drift not at these two sites (e.g. mantle's broader structural shape, scratch-file discipline elsewhere).
- New behavior; this is mechanical correctness-preserving fix.

## Verification

- bash -n clean across both files.
- regime-smoke / regime-validation no regressions vs. pre-pace baseline (same marshal-zero failures expected, nothing new).
- Pristine-tier verification of `rbgp_governor_mantle` happens later via the BB heat's `rbw-tP` qualification pipeline; this pace doesn't gate on it.

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle GCP Governor - Project Orchestration
20	
21	
22	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
23	# Operational Invariants (RBGG is single writer; 409 is fatal)
24	#
25	# - Single admin actor: All RBGG operations are executed by a single admin
26	#   identity. There are no concurrent writers in the same project.
27	# - Pristine-state expectation: RBGG init/creation flows assume the project
28	#   is pristine for the resources they manage. If a resource "already exists"
29	#   (HTTP 409), that's treated as state drift or prior manual activity.
30	# - Policy: All HTTP 409 Conflict responses are fatal (buc_die). We do not
31	#   treat 409 as idempotent success anywhere in RBGG.
32	#   If you see a 409, resolve state drift first (destroy/reset), then rerun.
33	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
34	
35	set -euo pipefail
36	
37	# Multiple inclusion detection
38	test -z "${ZRBGG_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgg multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
39	ZRBGG_SOURCED=1
40	
41	######################################################################
42	# Internal Functions (zrbgg_*)
43	
44	zrbgg_kindle() {
45	  test -z "${ZRBGG_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgg already kindled"
46	
47	  test -n "${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID:-}"     || buc_die "RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID is not set"
48	  test   "${#RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" -gt 0 || buc_die "RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID is empty"
49	
50	  buc_log_args 'Ensure dependencies are kindled first'
51	  zrbgc_sentinel
52	  zrbgo_sentinel
53	  zrbgu_sentinel
54	  zrbgi_sentinel
55	
56	  readonly ZRBGG_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgg_"
57	  readonly ZRBGG_EMPTY_JSON="${ZRBGG_PREFIX}empty.json"
58	  printf '{}' > "${ZRBGG_EMPTY_JSON}"
59	
60	  # Infix values for HTTP operations
61	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE="create"
62	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_VERIFY="verify"
63	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_KEY="key"
64	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_API_IAM_ENABLE="api_iam_enable"
65	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_API_CRM_ENABLE="api_crm_enable"
66	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_API_ART_ENABLE="api_art_enable"
67	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_API_BUILD_ENABLE="api_build_enable"
68	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_CB_SA_ACCOUNT_GEN="cb_account_gen"
69	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_CB_PRIME="cb_prime"
70	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_API_CONTAINERANALYSIS_ENABLE="api_containeranalysis_enable"
71	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_API_STORAGE_ENABLE="api_storage_enable"
72	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_INFO="project_info"
73	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE_REPO="create_repo"
74	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_VERIFY_REPO="verify_repo"
75	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_DELETE_REPO="delete_repo"
76	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_REPO_POLICY="repo_policy"
77	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_RPOLICY_SET="repo_policy_set"
78	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_ROSTER="roster"
79	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_API_CHECK="api_checking"
80	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_DELETE="delete"
81	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_KEYS="list_keys"
82	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_CREATE="bucket_create"

84	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_LIST="bucket_list"
85	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_OBJECT_DELETE="object_delete"
86	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_LIENS="list_liens"
87	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_DELETE="project_delete"
88	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_STATE="project_state"
89	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_RESTORE="project_restore"
90	
91	  readonly ZRBGG_KINDLED=1
92	}
93	
94	zrbgg_sentinel() {
95	  test "${ZRBGG_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbgg not kindled - call zrbgg_kindle first"
96	}
97	
98	# Path-driven sibling of rbgu_json_field_capture for files that live outside
99	# the rbgu cache (under RBRR_SECRETS_DIR/assay/* for credential bytes that
100	# must not leak into BURD_TEMP_DIR).
101	zrbgg_secrets_json_field_capture() {
102	  zrbgg_sentinel
103	  local -r z_path="${1}"
104	  local -r z_jq="${2}"
105	  local z_result
106	  z_result=$(jq -r "${z_jq}" "${z_path}")  || return 1
107	  if test -z "${z_result}" || test "${z_result}" = "null"; then return 1; fi
108	  echo "${z_result}"
109	}
110	
111	######################################################################
112	######################################################################
113	# Capture: list required services that are NOT enabled (blank = all enabled)
114	zrbgg_required_apis_missing_capture() {
115	  zrbgg_sentinel
116	
117	  local z_token="${1:-}"
118	  test -n "${z_token}" || { echo ""; return 1; }
119	
120	  local z_missing=""
121	  local z_api=""
122	  local z_service=""
123	  local z_infix=""
124	  local z_state=""
125	  local z_code=""
126	
127	  for z_api in                       \
128	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_CRM}"      \
129	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_GAR}"      \
130	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_IAM}"      \
131	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_BUILD}"    \
132	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_ANALYSIS}" \
133	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_STORAGE}"
134	  do
135	    z_service="${z_api##*/}"
136	    z_infix="${ZRBGG_INFIX_API_CHECK}_${z_service}"
137	
138	    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_api}" "${z_token}" "${z_infix}" || true
139	
140	    buc_log_args 'If we cannot even read an HTTP code file, that is a processing failure.'
141	    z_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_infix}") || z_code=""
142	    test -n "${z_code}" || return 1
143	
144	    if test "${z_code}" = "200"; then
145	      z_state=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" ".state") || z_state=""
146	      test "${z_state}" = "ENABLED" || z_missing="${z_missing} ${z_service}"
147	    else
148	      buc_log_args 'Any non-200 (403/404/5xx/etc) => treat as NOT enabled'
149	      z_missing="${z_missing} ${z_service}"
150	    fi
151	  done
152	
153	  printf '%s' "${z_missing# }"
154	}
155	
156	zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key() {
157	  zrbgg_sentinel
158	
159	  local z_account_name="$1"
160	  local z_display_name="$2"
161	  local z_description="$3"
162	  local z_role="$4"
163	
164	  local z_account_email="${z_account_name}@${RBGD_SA_EMAIL_FULL}"
165	
166	  buc_step 'Get OAuth token from admin'
167	  local z_token
168	  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"
169	
170	  buc_step "Create request JSON for ${z_account_name}"
171	  jq -n                                      \
172	    --arg account_id "${z_account_name}"     \
173	    --arg display_name "${z_display_name}"   \
174	    --arg description "${z_description}"     \
175	    '{
176	      accountId: $account_id,
177	      serviceAccount: {
178	        displayName: $display_name,
179	        description: $description
180	      }
181	    }' > "${ZRBGG_PREFIX}create_request.json" || buc_die "Failed to create request JSON"
182	
183	  buc_step 'Create service account via REST API'
184	  rbgu_http_json "POST" "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}" "${z_token}" \
185	    "${ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE}" "${ZRBGG_PREFIX}create_request.json"
186	  rbgu_http_require_ok "Create service account" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE}"
187	
188	  local z_sa_uid
189	  z_sa_uid=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE}" '.uniqueId') \
190	    || buc_die "Failed to get uniqueId from SA creation response"
191	  buc_info "Service account created: ${z_account_email} (uid: ${z_sa_uid})"
192	
193	  rbgu_poll_until_ok "SA propagation (by email)" \
194	    "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}/${z_account_email}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_VERIFY}"
195	
196	  buc_step 'Preflight: ensure no existing USER_MANAGED keys (manual cleanup path)'
197	
198	  buc_log_args 'List keys'

Tabtargets swap (delete four old rbw-a[CKLF].*.sh, create six new rbw-a[rd][IDr].*.sh with subject-verb-object frontispieces GovernorInvestsRetriever/etc). E. rbgp_depot_unmake fold (paginated governor-* SA cleanup with 404 tolerance via rbgu_http_require_ok pattern, BCG-compliant inline implementation, placed before lien removal so cleanup runs while project still has quota; mantle's existing pre-cleanup left BCG-non-compliant for separate scope).
